researched_category.notes: Ramp provides corporate cards, expense management, bill payments, procurement, and treasury services with AI-driven automation.

one_liner.value: Corporate card, expense management, and bill payment platform with a public API for programmatic spend management and AI agent integration
one_liner.notes: Ramp positions itself as a finance automation platform for tracking business expenses, managing cards, and processing bills.

methods.notes: Ramp uses OAuth 2.0 with Client Credentials (primary for server-to-server/agent) and Authorization Code (third-party apps) grants. The /developer/v1/token endpoint uses HTTP Basic Auth (client_id:client_secret base64-encoded) to issue Bearer access tokens.

access.notes: Anyone can sign up for Ramp Free ($0/mo). API credentials require workspace admin access within a Ramp account. Sandbox access is not self-serve (must contact account manager). Production API access is available to any Ramp customer with admin privileges. Partner integrations require an application/review process for production listing.

summary.value: Ramp has a comprehensive documented REST API (docs.ramp.com) covering transactions, cards, users, bills, reimbursements, vendors, accounting, departments, locations, custom records, spend controls, banking, procurement, and more. The API uses OAuth 2.0 scopes for granular permissions. An OpenAPI schema is available at /openapi/developer-api.json. Webhooks are supported for event-driven workflows. Rate limits apply.
summary.notes: The API surface is very broad with many endpoints documented in the API reference (llms-api.txt shows dozens of endpoints).

agent_interface.notes: Ramp has an official MCP server (ramp-public/ramp_mcp, MIT license on GitHub) that works with Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP clients. They also have a CLI tool, explicit AI agent guides, and a Composio toolkit with 88 tools.

rationale.value: Ramp is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API with broad coverage, an official MIT-licensed MCP server, a CLI tool with agent mode, explicit documentation for AI agent integration patterns, and a Composio toolkit already available (88 tools). Authentication is straightforward (OAuth2 Client Credentials). Any developer can sign up for a free Ramp account and start building immediately. The only minor friction is that sandbox environments require contacting an account manager, but production API access is available through the dashboard.
rationale.notes: No material blockers exist. Ramp has explicitly designed for AI agent use cases with MCP support and agent cards.

s1.quote: Spend Management, Corporate Cards & Accounts Payable Solutions | Ramp
s1.title: Ramp Homepage

s2.quote: Ramp's Developer API allows teams to programmatically access Ramp data and build automations or integrations tailored to their workflows.

s3.quote: Use Ramp at your company, or build for others. For customers: Have a Ramp account? Create credentials and make your first API call.
s3.title: Ramp API Docs - Getting Started

s4.quote: Ramp uses OAuth 2.0 for secure API access... Ramp authenticates requests to /developer/v1/token using your client ID and client secret, typically with HTTP Basic Auth.
s4.title: Authorization Guide

s5.quote: Ramp authenticates this /developer/v1/token request with HTTP Basic Auth: base64-encode client_id:client_secret and send it in the Authorization header.
s5.title: Quickstart Guide

s6.quote: Access tokens issued by Ramp have the following characteristics: Format: Opaque tokens (not JWT), Lifetime: 10 days (864,000 seconds) for Client Credentials
s6.title: Authorization Guide - Token Management

s7.quote: Sandbox access is not self-serve. To get a sandbox environment, contact your account manager.
s7.title: Sandbox Environment

s8.quote: A Model Context Protocol server for retrieving and analyzing data or running tasks for Ramp using Developer API.
s8.title: ramp-public/ramp_mcp

path: /developer/v1/accounting/accounts

s12.quote: AI Agents - Give an AI agent read access, action authority, and (with approval) real purchasing power on Ramp

limitations: Sandbox access requires contacting account manager (not self-serve)
Some sensitive scopes like cards:read_vault require additional security review
Agent Cards feature requires separate application (agents.ramp.com/cards)
